---
title: "Research Instruments"
subtitle: "Coding Scheme, Survey, Task Briefs, and Nudge Variants"
format:
  html: default
format-links: false
---

## Measurement Instruments

| Document | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| [Coding Scheme](coding-scheme.qmd) | Transcript coding scheme (v2.0) - FU, CH, EX dimensions, dual scoring, mixed-effects analysis spec |
| [Exit Survey](exit-survey.qmd) | Qualtrics exit survey (v2.0) - confidence calibration, self-reported interaction style, prior AI experience |
| [Nudge Variants](nudge-variants.qmd) | Pool of 10 nudge variants randomly selected per turn |

## Task Briefs - Conversational (Primary)

These tasks require iterative dialogue to develop a recommendation. No single right answer.

| Document | Discipline | Co-I |
|----------|-----------|------|
| [Information Systems](task-brief-information-systems.qmd) | IS (ISYS6018, ~50 students) | Michael Borck |
| [Supply Chain Management](task-brief-supply-chain.qmd) | SCM (MGMT6076, ~30 students) | Torsten Reiners |
| [Marketing](task-brief-marketing.qmd) | Marketing (MKTG1000, ~220 students) | Marcela Moraes |
| [People and Culture](task-brief-people-culture.qmd) | HR (MGMT3014, ~800-1000 students) | Renee Ralph |

## Task Briefs - Retrieval-Framed (Follow-on)

These tasks appear to require factual retrieval, where AI responses are most likely to be confidently wrong. Designed for a planned follow-on study.

| Document | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| [Retrieval-Framed Variants](task-brief-retrieval-framed.qmd) | One variant per discipline - privacy compliance, shipping regulations, advertising law |
